programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy, Freddie and Chris set out on a high-altitude voyage from Kathmandu to the Forbidden City of Lo Manthang, through the highest mountains, deepest gorges and harshest terrain on the planet.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are dropped deep in the Bolivian rain forest armed only with three 4x4s which they bought from the local small ads for a maximum of £3,500 each. They attempt to drive from the heart of Bolivia to the coast of Chile, encountering drug lords and the debilitating effects of high altitude. Without a doubt one of the toughest challenges the `Top Gear team has ever faced.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team hit Norway for a Winter Olympics special in which they attempt Olympic events, but with cars. In a world first, the team fire a rocket-powered Mini off a ski jump, Jeremy and James tackle the biathlon with the latest 4x4s, Richard stages a game of car ice hockey, and the new Jaguar XK races a speed skater.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May head to Vietnam to travel the entire length of the country in just eight days, thereby achieving what the American military failed to do in ten years. First they must each buy some suitable transport for the trip and, despite having several million Dong in their pockets, this proves to be rather more difficult than they thought.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy, Richard and James face their greatest challenge to date as all three are sent to Africa and each told to buy a second-hand car costing no more than £1,500. It must not be a 4x4 and must not be designed in any way to go off-road. Which could be a problem, since their task is then to drive across the wilds of Botswana, negotiating massive salt pans, treacherous rivers and animal reserves.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Best of British
programme.desc: The Bikers explore how some of the foodie customs associated with the wedding ceremony have evolved and help prepare a Scottish wedding breakfast. The programme also explores the traditions behind the wedding cake.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon is back in the hosts chair for the award-winning comedy panel show, with Lee Mack and David Mitchell returning as the lightning-quick team captains. Each week, a stellar cast of celebrity guests reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up? Sorting the fact from fiction this time are guests Charlie Brooker, Professor Hannah Fry, Danny Jones and Shaparak Khorsandi.

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Gemma Cairney, Tim Key, Gabby Logan and Jeff Stelling take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman.

programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants David Baddiel, Alex Brooker, Sophie Duker and Charlotte Hawkins take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman.

programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig goes to university with Guz Khan, Joe Lycett, Morgana Robinson and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Frankie Boyles New World Order
programme.desc: In an attempt to make sense of the bewildering world we live in, Frankie Boyle dissects the weeks news using stand-up, review, discussion and audience interaction.

programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts with panellists rail union leader Mick Lynch and comedian Roisin Conaty joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news.

programme.desc: Guests include James Acaster, Ed Byrne, Gary Delaney and Sara Pascoe.

programme.title: Red Dwarf

Passing through the leak, the crew are able to go back in time to a point before the rest of the crew were wiped out and might just be able to bring someone back with them.

programme.title: Ill Get This
programme.desc: Five celebrities meet at a restaurant for a fancy meal. The catch is they have to play games between courses and the loser will have to pay for everyones dinner. Featuring Josh Widdicombe, Gemma Collins, Adrian Chiles, Melvin ODoom and Holly Walsh.

programme.title: Ill Get This

programme.title: Gadget Man
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade turns his attention to the noise, dirt and discomfort on public transport. Russell Howard joins Richard on Londons tube network to assess the effectiveness of gadgets designed to deal with poor air quality and extreme heat.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Best of British
programme.desc: Whether savoury or sweet, the British love puddings. Dave and Si create some classic puds and explore the history and traditions behind our favourites. There is no point in counting calories with delicious puds, but the bikers do try and burn some off when they take part in the world black-pudding throwing competition in Lancashire.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The hosts set out to build a bridge over the River Kwai in Thailand, but must first drive across Burma. Jeremy, Richard and James then drive three ancient, barely functioning trucks as they head toward their final challenge.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May compare the Mercedes SLS, the Porsche 911 GT3 RS and the Ferrari 458 Italia in an epic road trip up Americas east coast. Along the way they undertake a series of challenges culminating in a fight to find the fastest way into New York, all in the name of crowning the worlds greatest sports car. Plus, another celebrity takes to the track as The Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: In this special Christmas episode, Freddie, Chris and Paddy play car secret Santa for a festive road trip across Britain, transporting a giant Christmas tree as they head to Top Gears very own winter wonderland.

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Hollys increasingly erratic behaviour endangers Lister and the ship, and backup computer Queeg 500 is brought in to replace him, relegating Holly to the role of night watchman. Unfortunately, Queeg has peculiarities all of his own, so the crew end up challenging Holly to take him on. Queeg chooses a game of chess and the loser faces deletion.

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Holly devises a faster-than-light drive known as the `Holly Hop Drive which can transport the Red Dwarf anywhere in the universe in a microsecond. programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts with panellists rail union leader Mick Lynch and comedian Roisin Conaty joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news.

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Guests include James Acaster, Ed Byrne, Gary Delaney and Sara Pascoe.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for an episode of previously unseen material from the hit comedy panel show.

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig takes a look at upbringing with Sally Phillips, Josh Pugh, Aaron Simmonds and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Lister decides to read Rimmers diary and discovers a leak from the `Stasis (suspended-animation chamber) that creates a doorway to the past. Passing through the leak, the crew are able to go back in time to a point before the rest of the crew were wiped out and might just be able to bring someone back with them.

programme.title: Ill Get This
programme.desc: Five celebrities meet at a restaurant for a fancy meal. The catch is they have to play games between courses and the loser will have to pay for everyones dinner. Featuring Josh Widdicombe, Gemma Collins, Adrian Chiles, Melvin ODoom and Holly Walsh.

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: While Syed wonders if Masood saw him with Christian, a terrified Ben finds other ways to get away from his bullies, and Ronnie is chased by a handsome doctor.

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: The whole practice is saddened by the death of King George VI. Meanwhile, Siegfried is determined to heal the rift that has developed between Calum and Deirdre.

programme.title: Howards Way
programme.desc: Avril is concerned about a take over bid when she learns that Ken has sold Charles his shares. Following his overdose, James life hangs in the balance.

programme.desc: An idyllic Shakespeare walk soon turns to disaster when one of the walkers is murdered and Frank becomes the prime suspect.

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon is confident that the people of Chigwell will be crying out for her pet care services, and Tracey starts doing a spot of typing from home.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Someone isnt getting his round in at the local pub, and something must be done about it.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo gets on his bike - motorbike, that is - and tries to out-perform Evel Knievel.

programme.title: Father Brown

programme.title: Happy Valley

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: An eccentric group of ex-police officers are brought out of retirement to investigate unsolved crimes. Jack confronts his nemesis in court when Ricky Hanson stands trial for his attempted murder, and Gerry faces the consequences of lying to his `daughter Emily. The team uncover a shady organ donor racket when they reinvestigate the murder of a wealthy businessman who left a £3 million fortune to a prostitute.

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: When a key witness is murdered, Lynley and Havers find their case against violent criminal Michael Shand crumbling around them. Determined to see him brought down, they are forced into a dark world where a family seaside town masks a sinister underworld and Havers is forced to confront her childhood memories.

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: A strange young man turns up asking for help when his car radiator springs a leak, but Sharon wonders if he is a police spy getting information on Chris and Darryl and regrets talking too much.

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A visit to a Gothic house in Scotland brings Lovejoy and Lady Jane closer together. Meanwhile, Eric falls in love, and Tinker finds himself all at sea.

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The bailiffs have finally caught up with the old rogue Lovejoy after a dawn raid leaves him with virtually nothing. His only salvation appears to be a Napoleonic commode - but is it the real thing?

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: At a picture auction, Lovejoy tricks Lady Jane into taking part in an illegal bidding ring, a move that threatens their relationship.

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Father Brown senses something sinister afoot when Mrs McCarthys friend is the latest villager to die in her sleep.

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The UCOS team are drawn into a world of immigration loopholes, Albanian gangs and family feuds when they reinvestigate the death of an unidentified male discovered on farmland outside Waltham Forest in 1996, in what appeared to be a crucifixion.

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The UCOS team reinvestigates a highly sensitive case when new information links the unexplained death of a popular market trader, Kathy Green, to a series of drug rapes in East London.

programme.title: Jonathan Creek
programme.desc: Jonathan and Maddy are invited by Mimi Tranter, an old colleague of Maddys, to visit Ghosts Forge, the scene of an unsolved murder 18 months before. The reclusive owner of the house, Ezra Carr, was found stabbed and a burglary was suspected, although nothing had been stolen. What is the significance of the houses nameplate, a disappearing trick by Maddy, and a packet of books hidden in the attic?

programme.title: The Marlow Murder Club
programme.sub-title: An Appetite for Murder: Part One
programme.desc: When a popular but tempestuous celebrity chef is murdered during his cookbook launch, theres no shortage of suspects for Judith, Suzie and Becks.

programme.title: The Marlow Murder Club
programme.sub-title: An Appetite for Murder: Part Two
programme.desc: As police investigate the chefs death, a new development makes them rethink everything they have learned so far. Guest starring Susan Lynch.

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: With the assistance of Detective Kristin Sims, Detective Inspector Mike Shepherd investigates a wide range of murder mysteries in Brokenwood, a small town in the countryside of New Zealand.

programme.title: Whitechapel
programme.desc: Someone is killing witches in Whitechapel and Chandler is determined to catch them. As the mania of a witchhunt takes hold is there also a malign influence at work at the heart of the team?

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Lynley and Havers are called to Smithfields market where the body of a man has been found frozen on a meat truck. It appears that the victim was an illegal immigrant but was it an accidental death?

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Australia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon Reeve explores Australia. His adventure starts in the `red centre of the continent and onwards through South Australia via the extraordinary Indian Pacific Railway, until he reaches the west coast city of Perth. On the way, he joins an Aussie rancher in the parched outback and visits the lucrative tuna fishing city of Port Lincoln. Finally in Perth, Simon discovers a full scale British invasion.

programme.title: Australia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels from Darwin in Australias Northern Territory or `Top End, across to the remote Cape York Peninsula, and on to the Great Barrier Reef. Simon sets off from Kakadu, the largest national park in the world, where the wildlife is under threat from the march of millions of poisonous cane toads. He joins a team of toad-busters catching the amphibian invaders.

programme.title: Australia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels down the east coast of Australia. The final part of his journey begins in Surfers Paradise, near the city of Gold Coast. Then, in the East Coast suburbs, Simon joins a vet and his team who rescue and treat injured koalas. In Sydney, Simon investigates attitudes to immigration among Australians. Finally, Simon takes to the air as he travels to Melbourne - arriving just in time for Australia Day.

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Replaced at work by a box, Victor embarks on his enforced retirement. As an erstwhile security officer, he finds it exasperatingly difficult to adapt to this directionless new existence, and he begins magnifying mundane aspects of everyday life into major dramas.

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Margaret tries to help her husband cope with early retirement by getting him a book from the library entitled `Coping with Old Age. Maybe keep-fit classes at the local adult education centre will help take Victors mind off the depressing turn of events?

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Sardonic Victors life continues to be a trial - someone paints graffiti on the side of his house and the central heating system is making a deafening noise. To add to that theres a nasty smell coming from the living-room.

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor is convinced he is going insane after being trapped in a shed by a swarm of bees, he is stuck in the opticians for a day with his eyes shut, and then left to babysit some unruly children.

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor takes up drawing at a life class, but Margarets enthusiasm for his new hobby wanes when she discovers that he has given the model a lift home.

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.sub-title: The Return of the Speckled Band
programme.desc: Before the Meldrews can leave for their holiday in Athens, they must cope with Victors fear of flying, Mrs Warboys food poisoning, and a snake on the loose.

programme.desc: Replaced at work by a box, Victor embarks on his enforced retirement. As an erstwhile security officer, he finds it exasperatingly difficult to adapt to this directionless new existence, and he begins magnifying mundane aspects of everyday life into major dramas.

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Margaret tries to help her husband cope with early retirement by getting him a book from the library entitled `Coping with Old Age. Maybe keep-fit classes at the local adult education centre will help take Victors mind off the depressing turn of events?

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Sardonic Victors life continues to be a trial - someone paints graffiti on the side of his house and the central heating system is making a deafening noise. To add to that theres a nasty smell coming from the living-room.

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor is convinced he is going insane after being trapped in a shed by a swarm of bees, he is stuck in the opticians for a day with his eyes shut, and then left to babysit some unruly children.

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: (Widescreen) Mathew Horne, Joanna Page, James Corden, Ruth Jones, Larry Lamb, Alison Steadman. First ever episode of the much-loved sitcom that launched Smithy onto the scene! programme.desc: Things are starting to happen in Toms garden, but Barbara is feeling the effects of the boring and tiring work.

programme.desc: The wonderful Margo is horrified when she hears of Tom and Barbaras decision to keep pigs in the garden and enlists the help of the local residents association in an effort to scupper the plan.

programme.desc: When Tom, determined to make use of every available resource, builds a generator fuelled by pig dung, Margo and Jerry are not amused.

programme.desc: Agnes is upset that her son Trevor has been called back to the missions in Africa, but is determined to give him a massive send-off to remind him what a loving family he has back home in Ireland. Meanwhile, daughter Cathys family counselling session descends into chaos, and Buster and Dermot run into trouble with a circus lion. The only person having a good day is neighbour Winnie.

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: Baby fever hits the Brown household as a pregnant Maria prepares for motherhood, and Agnes is keen to give her new daughter-in-law her unique brand of advice about raising children. programme.desc: Pete helps a withdrawn pregnant Malinois regain strength so she can care for her 11 lively pups, and falls for Winter, a Staffie who is covered in scars.

After being phone buddies for months, Gavin and Stacey arrange a rendezvous in Leicester Square. (Subtitled, Audio Described)

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: After a huge misunderstanding on the phone, Stacey thinks Gavin has finished with her. He drives to Wales to explain and ends up taking her home with him. Having never been further than Bridgend before, her trip to Essex is an eye-opening experience that ends with the biggest surprise of all.

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: After his initial angry reaction to Staceys news, Uncle Bryn agrees to drive everyone to Essex to meet the Shipmans. Pam has laid on a cracking spread and the party goes with a bang, but then Gavin and Stacey make an announcement that throws all the bonhomie out the window.

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: The Death Cleaners help Lindsey after her mother and extended family leave everything they dont want at her house, causing her to lose her personal style.

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews...
programme.desc: Louis meets Oscar-winning actress Dame Judi Dench. Having graced the stage and screen for over 60 years, including iconic turns as Queen Elizabeth in Shakespeare in Love and MI5 chief M in Bond, Judi is regarded as one of Britains greatest ever actors. Louis meets Judi at her home in Surrey for a candid and lively conversation about her long career, her passion for theatre and her life away from acting.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillos Bradshaw travels resume in leafy Hertfordshire, where he attempts a canoe slalom course at the Lee Valley White Water Centre, built for the London Olympics in 2012.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Steered by his Bradshaws guide, Michael Portillo heads for the Hertfordshire village of Perry Green, where, in 1940, a young couple fleeing the bombing of London chose to make their home.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Suffolk on the last leg of his tour of East Anglia. He begins at the home of British horseracing, Newmarket, where he learns of the interwar success of a jockey turned trainer and helps to care for a famous ex-racehorse.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Crewe, a town steeped in railway history and immortalised in Victorian music hall, to investigate the making of the iconic cinema classic The Night Mail during the 1930s.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The team goes behind the scenes at Mathewsons, a North Yorkshire-based, family-run car auction business which uncovers rare vehicles in Thornton-le-Dale. It attracts classic car buyers from all over the country.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The team goes behind the scenes at Mathewsons, a North Yorkshire-based, family-run car auction business which uncovers rare vehicles in Thornton-le-Dale. It attracts classic car buyers from all over the country.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: Despite having the largest pre-war European army, Frances outdated forces were humiliated by the Germans. Identifying a weakness in the Maginot Line, a defensive border which ran along the north east of France but did not extend to the channel in the west, German air and armour power simply skirted the line in the north and forged south.

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: In 1940, Japan launched an attack on a Chinese city but they were not dropping bombs. Swarms of fleas descended and the city was infected with the bubonic plague.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team are at Sefton Park in Liverpool, where Chinese ceramics expert Lars Tharp sees a rare double Ming Dynasty vase bought in a charity shop, Raj Bisram discovers some valuable Beatles memorabilia, and Dendy Easton admires a painting by George Melly.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team visit Brodie Castle in the north of Scotland. Fiona Bruce meets a Highland Games commentator who has brought along a collection of items including a weighty shot put, a heavy hammer and a brooch presented by Queen Victoria in 1848.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The Restoring Classics team take Dereks advice when he sniffs out the rarest car theyve ever taken on - the last remaining Hillman GT.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team lift the shutters on their biggest restoration to date in the shape of a Series 1 Land Rover.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In the Welsh mountains of Snowdonia, Michael Portillo reaches an abandoned mine, where in 1940 the wartime government sought sanctuary for the National Gallerys priceless art collection.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Restoration Workshop

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie gets stuck in the mud in Woodseaves Cutting and explores the charming canal-side village of Kinver in Staffordshire.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie navigates the mighty River Severn and takes an unexpected bath as he tumbles at theTardebigge lock flight in Worcestershire.

programme.title: Dom Chineas Cornish Workshop
programme.desc: The workshop gets a new wall and floor. Meanwhile, Sam Lovegrove mends Doms vintage motorbike for a nail-biting cliff top trial. Plus, cider, an orchard and an auction are on the menu.

programme.title: Simon Reeves South America
programme.desc: Simon travels through three of the worlds most extreme environments: the salt flats of Bolivia, the Brazilian Pantanal and Paraguays Chaco Forest. In Bolivia, Simon meets a family who makes a living carving salt from the Uyuni salt flats.

programme.title: Simon Reeves South America
programme.desc: Simon visits Brazil, the biggest country on the continent. He starts in one of the remotest regions of the Amazon, with the Waiapi people, who cling on to their traditional way of life, which is under threat from logging and mining interests.

programme.title: David Jasons Great British Inventions
programme.desc: Sir David hears about Sir George Cayley and Percy Pilcher as he looks at the British inventors who played a part in the evolution of flight.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.desc: Derek picks up some very special working vehicles. Charlie feels the pull of a gargantuan Hummer and Paul extracts a vintage tractor from a Yorkshire barn.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A beautiful looking sports car has been fooling the punters and intriguing Derek. Its a Challenger E-Type - a fibreglass replica created from an E-Type mould.

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: The adventures of a family run crew of heavy-haulage specialists as they race to transport the heaviest, longest and most precious locomotives around the world by road, rail and sea. Plus, footage reveals the history behind the featured locomotives.

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: A fully restored Class 47 diesel engine is transported from its home at Crewe Heritage Centre in Cheshire to the Ecclesbourne Valley Railway in Derbyshire.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
